mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which reduces equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns average negative or zero when UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent across more than 30 observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirm broad-based demand expansion, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and PAYEMS produces a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 3-year rolling estimation window.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 100, supply-side capacity constraints bind, forcing price markups higher and delivering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns turn negative in more than 55 percent of instances where INDPRO remains above 100 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reduces the equity risk premium through improved corporate cash flow visibility, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.2 percent triggers inflation hedging flows and rate hike expectations, which compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.2 percent monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly rises in the unemployment rate and declines in commercial and industrial credit indicate a demand shock, which deteriorates earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising UNRATE and falling RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 in any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging above 1100 thousand units during a housing-led recovery phase signals strong construction demand and wealth effects, which immediately lift equity valuations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns average negative when HOUST breaks above 1100 thousand units across a 5-year backtest period.
